working_patterns: Hexagonal staggered row layout (5-4-5-4-5-3) exploits vertical sqrt(3)*r spacing to pack 26 circles efficiently
Computing r as min of horizontal constraint (10r<=1) and vertical constraint (2r + 5*sqrt(3)*r <= 1) ensures feasibility
Centering the pattern vertically with y0 = r + (1 - total_h)/2 uses available slack
Applying a small safety margin (r * 0.995) avoids floating-point validity rejections
failing_patterns: Using equal radii across all 26 circles leaves slack unused—especially the short row of 3 circles and the gap it creates
Hardcoded row_x0 offsets that start exactly at x=r touch the boundary and risk validity failures without margins
The 0.995 margin uniformly shrinks all circles, sacrificing sum_radii even where circles aren't boundary-limited
hypotheses: Score 0.920592 corresponds to sum ≈ 2.43 with r≈0.0934; known optimal for n=26 is ~2.635, so ~8% gap remains
Equal-radius constraint is suboptimal: the 3-circle row has huge unused space, so variable radii or repositioning could improve sum
The vertical constraint (2+5*sqrt(3))r binds tightly while horizontal has slack in 4-row and 3-row; asymmetry suggests non-uniform arrangement could help
A physics-based relaxation (expand radii + resolve overlaps) could escape the rigid hex-lattice local optimum
The 3-row is the weakest link—placing those 3 circles as larger corner/edge fillers could boost the sum
suggested_directions: Allow per-circle radii: keep hex positions as initialization, then run gradient/LP optimization maximizing sum(r_i) subject to pairwise and boundary constraints
Try alternative row decompositions: 6-5-5-5-5 (=26), 5-5-4-5-4-3, or 5-4-4-4-4-5 and compare achievable r
Implement physical simulation: initialize at hex layout, grow radii incrementally while projecting out overlaps and boundary violations
For the sparse 3-circle row, reposition those 3 circles to fill corners/edges with larger radii, treating them as independent fillers
Remove the 0.995 safety factor and instead compute exact r with tiny epsilon (1e-9) to preserve score
Try known-optimal configurations from Packomania for n=26 as a seed, then locally optimize
Use scipy.optimize with constraints: variables are (x_i, y_i, r_i) for all 26 circles, maximize sum(r_i)
